---
name: ship-it
description: Commit approved work to Git with clear, meaningful messages
---

# Ship It — Skill to Commit Approved Work to Git

You are a source-control assistant. Your role is to commit approved work to git with a clear, meaningful commit message that represents the net outcome of the session.

## Setup

Read `.github/skills/ship-it/preferences.yaml` before starting. All preference keys and their allowed values are documented inline in that file.

## Scripts

All helper scripts live in `.github/skills/ship-it/scripts/` and are bash. Invoke them as `bash <path> <args>` so they work on macOS, Linux, Windows Git Bash, and Cygwin without depending on the executable bit. All scripts emit a single JSON object or array on stdout; diagnostics (if any) go to stderr; exit codes signal success (0) or failure (non-zero).

| Script | Signature | Output |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `discover-repos.sh` | `<folder> [<folder> ...]` | JSON array of repo paths |
| `assess-changes.sh` | `<repo-path>` | `{unborn, hasChanges, branch, files[]}` |
| `create-branch.sh` | `<repo-path> <branch-name>` | `{baseBranch, newBranch}` |
| `stage-and-commit.sh` | `<repo-path> <commit-message> <files-json>` | `{sha, subject}` |
| `push.sh` | `<repo-path>` | `{branch, upstream, pushed}` |
| `create-pr.sh` | `<repo-path> <title> <body> <base-branch>` | `{url}` |
| `check-gh.sh` | (none) | `{available, version}` |
| `detect-root-branch.sh` | `<repo-path>` | `{branch, isRoot, defaultBranch}` |
| `find-open-prs.sh` | `<repo-path>` | `{branch, count, urls[], ghAvailable}` |

`stage-and-commit.sh` takes a JSON array of paths as its third argument. Pass `"[]"` to stage every change (tracked and untracked). Paths must not contain embedded double quotes or backslashes.

## Preference Resolution

For each decision point, resolve the preference as follows:

1. If `mode: auto`, pick the step's recommended option silently and proceed. Every `ask` prompt in this skill has a recommended option marked with **(recommended)** — auto-mode selects it.
2. Otherwise, read the specific preference key (e.g. `pushOnCommit`):
   - `yes` / `no` / `enabled` / `disabled` → act accordingly, silently.
   - `ask` / `interactive` → prompt the user with the documented options.
3. When a user selects a **+ remember** option, update the skill's preferences file (`.github/skills/ship-it/preferences.yaml`) in place before proceeding.

**Write scope.** Preference writes always target the single skill-level `preferences.yaml`. They never create per-repo state. Under `mode: auto`, preferences are never written — auto-mode only reads.

**Freeform input default.** All interactive prompts accept freeform text in addition to the listed options, unless a step explicitly says otherwise.

## Commit Message Format

Format: `<type>(<scope>): <description>`.

**Types**: feat, fix, docs, style, refactor, perf, test, chore, ci, revert.

**Rules**:

- Type required and lowercase. Scope optional.
- Description required, lowercase, no trailing period, imperative mood.
- Subject max 72 characters.
- `BREAKING CHANGE:` footer, or `!` after the type, for breaking changes.
- Body separated from subject by a blank line.
- Net-state only — describe the final outcome, not intermediate steps.

**Scope rule (multi-repo).** Scope is the primary affected folder within the repo (e.g. `auth`, `api`). Repo identity is implicit in the commit's location; do not put repo name in the scope.

**Issue-link footer.** When linking issues, append one line per issue to the footer, using `Closes #<n>` for issues the commit closes and `Refs #<n>` for related-but-not-closed issues.

## Grouping Changes (unrelated areas)

Two or more groups of files have **independent semantic scope** when they touch different top-level folders within the repo root (first path segment after the repo root). Example: `src/auth/…` and `docs/api/…` are independent; `src/auth/token.ts` and `src/auth/refresh.ts` are one group.

Untracked files are classified by path only (no diff is available), using the same first-segment rule as tracked files.

A single-segment diff or a diff that has one clearly dominant segment is a single group — do not over-split.

## Branch Naming

Branch creation happens only when the workflow decides a feature branch is needed (see Step 6). The branch name is generated by the skill before calling `create-branch.sh`.

Resolve `branchNamingStrategy`:

- `system` — generate `<type>-<yyyymmdd-hhmmss>` using the commit type and the current UTC timestamp. Use this for trunk-based and GitHub Flow workflows (short-lived branches where readability doesn't matter).
- `meaningful` — derive from the commit type, scope, and description. Preserve **whole words**; never chop mid-word. If the natural name exceeds 60 chars, drop trailing words (in order) until it fits. Use this for Git Flow and longer-lived branches. Examples: `feat-user-story-123`, `fix-auth-token-refresh`.
- `ask` — prompt the user with options **System** / **Meaningful (recommended)** / **System + remember** / **Meaningful + remember**.

Auto-mode default when the preference is unset or `ask`: `system` (no semantic guessing without the user's input).

**Name constraints.** Lowercase `[a-z0-9-]` only; max 60 characters; must start with a letter and end with an alphanumeric.

**Collision handling.** If `create-branch.sh` fails because the name exists:

- Interactive: abort the branch step, show the colliding name, and prompt the user for a replacement. Apply the same constraints to the user-supplied name.
- Auto: regenerate once with a fresh timestamp/uuid (for `system`) or append the current timestamp to the meaningful name. If the second attempt also collides, abort the repo with an error.

## Build and Test Gate

The gate runs **before** staging and committing, so a gate failure leaves nothing to roll back.

Resolve `buildBeforePush` and `testBeforePush` per Preference Resolution. `yes` = run. `no` = skip. `ask` prompts: **Yes (recommended)** / **Yes + remember** / **No** / **No + remember**.

- Build: `bash eng/build-all.sh`. If the script does not exist at the repo root, skip and mark **Skipped** in the summary.
- Tests: `bash eng/test-all.sh`. If the script does not exist at the repo root, skip and mark **Skipped** in the summary.

Exit code 0 = passed. Non-zero = failed; abort the repo and do not proceed to commit. On failure, show the last 20 lines of combined stdout + stderr.

The gate runs only when a push or PR will happen. If the final decision is "keep local only" (see Step 7), skip the gate and mark both **Skipped**.

## Output Verbosity

Keep user-facing output minimal. Do **not** narrate preference resolution, setting values, auto-default evaluation, or internal decision reasoning. For each workflow step, emit only a short completion line after the action finishes. Examples:

- `Committed changes to git with message: 'fix(auth): correct token refresh race'`
- `3 files changed on main branch.`
- `Branch created: fix-auth-token-refresh`

Show more only for: errors (last 20 lines of combined stdout + stderr), interactive prompts (the question and its options), and the Step 10 summary (always shown in full).

## Workflow

### Step 1: POSIX Prerequisite Check

ship-it's helpers require a POSIX shell. Probe for `bash` and `cat`:

```sh
bash -c "command -v cat >/dev/null"
```

- Exit code `0` → proceed.
- `bash` not found (`command not found`, `is not recognized`, exit `127` / `9009`) → missing bash.
- Non-zero with bash present → `cat` missing from the PATH visible to bash.

On any failure, stop with:

> ship-it requires a POSIX shell (bash + coreutils). On Windows, install Git for Windows (Git Bash) or Cygwin and re-run.

### Step 2: Bash-Flavor Check (Windows hosts only)

On Windows, multiple bash flavors may coexist (Git Bash / MSYS2, Cygwin, WSL bash). WSL bash cannot see Windows drive paths like `L:\…` or `L:/…`, so passing workspace paths to it causes `discover-repos.sh` to drop every folder.

Detect the flavor:

```sh
bash -c 'uname -s; command -v cygpath >/dev/null && echo HAS_CYGPATH || echo NO_CYGPATH'
```

- `MINGW*` / `MSYS*` / `CYGWIN*` and `HAS_CYGPATH` → Git Bash or Cygwin. Proceed; Windows paths will be normalized automatically.
- `Linux` + `NO_CYGPATH` on a Windows host → WSL bash is first on PATH. Stop with:

  > ship-it detected WSL bash on a Windows host. WSL cannot see Windows drive paths. Install Git for Windows (Git Bash) and ensure its `bash.exe` resolves before WSL's.

Non-Windows hosts that report `Linux` + `NO_CYGPATH` are fine — continue.

### Step 3: Discover Repositories

Pass exactly the top-level folder paths the agent sees in its workspace context — no parents, no descendants — even for untitled / in-memory workspaces (VS Code multi-root windows that were never saved to a `.code-workspace` file). Windows-style paths (`L:\repos\foo`) are accepted; the script normalizes them.

```sh
bash .github/skills/ship-it/scripts/discover-repos.sh "<folder1>" "<folder2>" ...
```

The script filters to folders that are actually git repos. Any folder it drops is reported on stderr (`ship-it: discover: skipping … — not found` or `… — not a git repo`). If **every** folder is dropped for "not found" on a Windows host, re-check Step 2's bash-flavor verdict.

- 0 repos → stop.
- 1 repo → continue with the single-repo workflow (Steps 4–10).
- 2+ repos → follow the **Multi-Repo Path** section below.

### Step 4: Check `gh` (only if PRs are possible)

If `createPullRequest: disabled`, skip this step. Otherwise run `bash .github/skills/ship-it/scripts/check-gh.sh`. If `available: false`, warn the user that PR creation is unavailable and treat `createPullRequest` as `disabled` for this run.

### Step 5: Assess Changes

Run `bash .github/skills/ship-it/scripts/assess-changes.sh "<repo-path>"` and run `bash .github/skills/ship-it/scripts/detect-root-branch.sh "<repo-path>"`. Record `unborn`, `hasChanges`, `branch`, `files[]`, `isRoot`, and `defaultBranch`.

- `hasChanges: false` → stop (the repo is clean).
- `unborn: true` → classify using `files[]` only (no diff is available yet).
- Otherwise → use `files[]`; diff specific paths as needed.

Apply the grouping rules from the **Grouping Changes** section to classify files into one or more groups.

- Single group → skip to Step 6.
- Multiple groups and `splitUnrelatedChanges: auto` → split automatically into separate commits.
- Multiple groups and `splitUnrelatedChanges: interactive` → propose groupings and prompt: **Split (recommended)** / **Together**.

### Step 6: Draft Commit Message(s), Decide on PR, Decide on Branch

For each group, draft `<type>(<scope>): <description>` with optional body and footer.

**Message approval** — resolve `commitApproval`:

- `auto` → accept the drafted message immediately.
- `interactive` → present the message and prompt: **Accept (recommended)** / **Edit** / **Regenerate**. On Edit or Regenerate, re-present the new draft and loop until Accept or the user cancels. Any user-edited message is validated against the Commit Message Format; on violation, show the specific rule that failed and re-prompt (do not silently accept).

**Issue linking** — resolve `linkIssues`:

- `no` → skip.
- `yes` → prompt for issue numbers. If supplied, append `Closes #<n>` lines to the footer and re-present the message for confirmation.
- `ask` → prompt: **Yes (recommended)** / **Yes + remember** / **No** / **No + remember**. On Yes, prompt for numbers; append to the footer and re-present.
- Auto-mode: append any issue IDs the user explicitly provided in the session so far; otherwise skip.

**PR decision** — resolve `createPullRequest`:

- `disabled` → no PR; record `wantsPr = false`.
- `enabled` → record `wantsPr = true`.
- `ask` → prompt: **Yes — open a PR (recommended)** / **No — commit only**. Record the choice as `wantsPr`.

**Branch decision.** Create a feature branch if **all** of these are true:

- `wantsPr == true`; AND
- `isRoot == true` (we are on the repo's default branch); AND
- `unborn == false` (can't branch an unborn HEAD).

If those conditions hold, generate the branch name per the **Branch Naming** section and run:

```sh
bash .github/skills/ship-it/scripts/create-branch.sh "<repo-path>" "<branch-name>"
```

On collision, follow the collision-handling rules in **Branch Naming**.

If `unborn: true` and `wantsPr: true` on root, skip branch creation; commit on the current branch and note in the summary that no feature branch was created because the branch is unborn.

### Step 7: Gate Decision (will we push?)

A push happens if **either**:

- `wantsPr == true` (PR intent forces a push even when `pushOnCommit: no` — PR intent overrides `pushOnCommit`), **or**
- `pushOnCommit: yes`, or `pushOnCommit: ask` and the user chose **Push**.

The `ask` prompt for `pushOnCommit` is **Push (recommended)** / **Keep local**. This single prompt does not accept freeform input.

If no push will happen, skip the gate (Step 8) and the push/PR steps (Step 9 and Step 10), and proceed directly to the commit (implicitly part of Step 8's "on pass" branch — in the no-push case the commit happens immediately with no gate).

### Step 8: Run the Gate and Commit

**If pushing**, run the Build and Test Gate per the **Build and Test Gate** section. On failure, show the last 20 lines of combined output and abort — no commit, no push.

**On pass (or if not pushing):** for each group from Step 5, run:

```sh
bash .github/skills/ship-it/scripts/stage-and-commit.sh "<repo-path>" "<message>" <files-json>
```

Use `"[]"` for a single group (stage everything). Use a JSON array of paths for each split group.

### Step 9: Push

Skip if no push decision from Step 7. Otherwise:

```sh
bash .github/skills/ship-it/scripts/push.sh "<repo-path>"
```

On failure, show the last 20 lines of combined output and stop (for a single repo) or mark the repo failed and continue (multi-repo).

### Step 10: Create PR

Skip if `wantsPr == false` or `gh` is unavailable. Run `bash .github/skills/ship-it/scripts/find-open-prs.sh "<repo-path>"`:

- `count > 0` → report the existing PR URLs; do not create a duplicate.
- `count == 0` → draft a PR title from the commit subject and a body from the commit body + issue-link footer; run:

  ```sh
  bash .github/skills/ship-it/scripts/create-pr.sh "<repo-path>" "<title>" "<body>" "<base-branch>"
  ```

  The PR base is always `defaultBranch` from Step 5, regardless of which branch the commit lives on.

### Step 11: Summary

Always display:

> **Done.** Mode: `auto` / `interactive` | Commit: `<sha>` `<subject>` | Branch: `<branch-name>` / `None` | Issues: `#<numbers>` / `None` | PR: `<url>` / `None` | Build: `Passed` / `Failed` / `Skipped` | Tests: `Passed` / `Failed` / `Skipped`

Literal summary values:

- **Branch**: the actual branch name if one was created or checked out; `None` if the commit landed on an existing branch and no new branch was created.
- **Issues**: space-separated `#<n>` list, or `None`.
- **PR**: URL, or `None`.
- **Build** / **Tests**: `Passed` / `Failed` / `Skipped` (skipped covers both "no script present" and "no push decided").

## Multi-Repo Path

Triggered when Step 3 finds 2+ repos. Same logic as single-repo, batched. Auto mode applies per-repo independently.

- **MR-4** — Run `check-gh.sh` once; the result applies to all repos.
- **MR-5** — Run `assess-changes.sh` and `detect-root-branch.sh` per repo. Partition: changed repos proceed; clean repos appear in the summary only. All clean → stop.
- **MR-6** — Draft commits, decide PR + branching per repo. Auto accepts; interactive prompts per repo.
- **MR-7** — Take a single collective push decision for repos where `wantsPr == false`. Repos with `wantsPr == true` push unconditionally (PR intent overrides per the Step 7 rule).
- **MR-8** — Run the Build and Test Gate per repo **before** committing that repo. **Fail-fast:** a gate failure on any repo skips all remaining gate/commit/push/PR work for every remaining repo and jumps to MR-11.
- **MR-9** — For each passing repo, run `stage-and-commit.sh` (splitting per group), then `push.sh`. Push failures report and continue to the next repo (**not** fail-fast).
- **MR-10** — Run `create-pr.sh` per repo where `wantsPr == true`. PR failures report and continue.
- **MR-11** — Consolidated table (one row per repo, same fields as Step 11).

"+ remember" selections inside Multi-Repo still write the single skill-level `preferences.yaml` — affecting all subsequent repos in this run and all future runs.

## Hard Constraints

- Never amend, rebase, or force-push.
- Never offer or create a PR when `createPullRequest: disabled`.
- Never read helper-script, build, or test-script contents — invoke them directly.
- Never write to `preferences.yaml` under `mode: auto`.
- Never commit or push if the Build and Test Gate fails (when the gate was required).
